burial.plot_notes: Conneautville Cemetery, Conneautville Crawford County PA — with parents John Clarence + Gertha, wife Viola, and son Carl Arthur.

military_service.notes: World War II veteran per FA 'Veteran' designation. Specific unit, rank, theater, and dates of service undocumented in available sources.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost only son Carl at sixteen in 1965
narrative_blocks.body: Carmen and his wife Viola lost their only documented son Carl Arthur Ellis at sixteen on January 31, 1965 — twenty-two days after Carl's sixteenth birthday. The cause of his death is not recorded, but the proximity to that birthday milestone suggests illness or accident. Carmen lived another thirty-two years before his own death in 1997.

narrative_blocks.header: WWII veteran, born year after sister's daughter
narrative_blocks.body: Carmen Ellis was born May 20, 1920 in Erie Pennsylvania, less than two years after the 1918 influenza pandemic killed his second cousin Elvira Unger at age two. He served in World War II. He lived his post-war life in Crawford County Pennsylvania and was buried at Conneautville Cemetery alongside parents, wife, and son.

burial.plot_notes: Conneautville Cemetery, Conneautville Crawford County PA — with parents John Clarence + Gertha, wife Viola, and son Carl Arthur.

He served in World War II. He lived his post-war life in Crawford County Pennsylvania and was buried at Conneautville Cemetery alongside parents, wife, and son.

burial.plot_notes: Conneautville Cemetery, Conneautville Crawford County PA — with husband Carmen and son Carl Arthur.

narrative_blocks.header: Survived only son by fifty-five years
narrative_blocks.body: Viola Adams Ellis lost her only son Carl Arthur at sixteen in 1965, then her husband Carmen in 1997 — twenty-three years before her own 2020 death. She lived ninety-three years and was buried at Conneautville Cemetery alongside both husband and son, fifty-five years after Carl's death.

bio.photo_notes: Portrait of Carl Arthur Ellis (1949-1965), who died twenty-two days after his sixteenth birthday.

burial.plot_notes: Conneautville Cemetery, Conneautville Crawford County PA — first family member buried there ahead of his parents.

narrative_blocks.header: Died twenty-two days after his sixteenth birthday
narrative_blocks.body: Carl Arthur Ellis was born January 9, 1949 and died January 31, 1965 at age sixteen — twenty-two days after his sixteenth birthday. The cause of death is not documented in available sources, but the proximity to the birthday suggests illness or accident close to that milestone. He was the only documented child of Carmen and Viola Ellis.

narrative_blocks.header: His grave anchored the family Conneautville plot
narrative_blocks.body: Carl was buried at Conneautville Cemetery in January 1965 — the first of his immediate family interred there. His father Carmen joined him thirty-two years later in 1997, his mother Viola fifty-five years later in 2020. The family plot effectively built itself around his early grave.

burial.plot_notes: Conneautville Cemetery, Conneautville Crawford County PA.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Gertha E. Walton Ellis (1885-1973, age 88), m. 1904. 69-year marriage. Son Carmen A. Ellis (1920-1997, WWII vet). To be built Stage 6Y-91+.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost mother at one, lived to ninety-three
narrative_blocks.body: John Clarence Ellis was born October 5, 1884 at Meadville Pennsylvania to John Ellis's second wife Elsie Ann Jolly, who died of unknown causes when he was just over a year old. Despite this early loss he lived to ninety-three, dying December 15, 1977 at Conneautville Crawford County Pennsylvania — sixty-two years after his father.

narrative_blocks.header: Married Gertha Walton in 1904, sixty-nine years
narrative_blocks.body: John Clarence married Gertha Walton in 1904 when both were young — they would remain married for sixty-nine years until her death in 1973. Their only known son Carmen served in World War II. The family is buried at Conneautville Cemetery north of Meadville rather than at Greendale where his father, half-sister, and step-grandfather lie.

burial.plot_notes: Conneautville Cemetery, Conneautville Crawford County PA — with husband John Clarence Ellis.

narrative_blocks.header: Born four days before her husband's mother died
narrative_blocks.body: Gertha Walton was born February 22, 1885 in Summerhill Township Crawford County Pennsylvania — four days before her future husband John Clarence Ellis's mother Elsie Ann Jolly died on February 26 of that year. John was four months old at the moment Gertha was four days old. Nineteen years later they married and stayed married sixty-nine years until her death.

narrative_blocks.header: Sixty-nine year marriage ended four years before his
narrative_blocks.body: Gertha and John Clarence married in 1904 when both were nineteen. They had at least one son, Carmen, born when Gertha was thirty-five. She died November 25, 1973 at Meadville at age eighty-eight, four years before her husband's 1977 death at ninety-three.

burial.plot_notes: Greendale Cemetery, Meadville Crawford Co PA — same cemetery as his maternal great-uncle Dr. Edward Ellis MD's two wives Mary Kennedy and Sarah Buchanan.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Lois Olive Hibbard Ellis (1846-1871, died age 24) — to be built as I-prefix in Stage 6Y-90 along with daughter Florence Hannah Ellis McFarland (1864-1933).
Elsie Ann Jolly Ellis (1859-1885, died age 25-26) — to be built as I-prefix in Stage 6Y-90 along with son John Clarence Ellis (1884-1977).
Mrs. Florence Ellis (unknown-1930) — m. 1900, possibly already a widow with the surname Ellis at the time of marriage. To be built as I-prefix in Stage 6Y-90.

narrative_blocks.header: Daughter born Pithole forest before oil strike
narrative_blocks.body: John Ellis and his young wife Lois were living in the forested farmland of Venango County Pennsylvania when their only daughter Florence Hannah was born July 12, 1864 — six months before oil was struck at the nearby Frazier Well in January 1865. Within months the discovery exploded the area into Pithole City, a boomtown of fifteen thousand with fifty hotels, three churches, a daily newspaper, and the third-busiest post office in Pennsylvania. By 1870 the wells had run dry and Pithole was essentially a ghost town.

narrative_blocks.header: Followed Ellis family network west to Meadville
narrative_blocks.body: After leaving the collapsing Pithole oil settlement John Ellis moved his family to Meadville Pennsylvania, where his maternal great-uncle Dr. Edward Ellis MD had practiced medicine for forty years already. He lived in Meadville the rest of his life — over four decades — and was buried at Greendale Cemetery alongside the graves of Edward's two deceased wives, who had died in 1840 and 1844.

narrative_blocks.header: Survived three wives, two died young
narrative_blocks.body: John Ellis was widowed three times: first wife Lois Olive Hibbard died at twenty-four in 1871 leaving him their daughter Florence Hannah aged seven, and second wife Elsie Ann Jolly died at twenty-six in 1885 leaving him their year-old son John Clarence. He married a third time in 1900 at age sixty to a widow surnamed Ellis.

narrative_blocks.header: Only surviving child of first-cousin parents
narrative_blocks.body: John was the only one of his parents Hannah and Noah Ellis's four children to marry and have descendants. His older sister Sarah died unmarried at twenty-four in 1871, and his infant twin sisters Mary and Martha had both died in 1851.

burial.plot_notes: McDowell Cemetery, Dicksonburg Crawford County PA.

narrative_blocks.header: Died young like first wife, leaving infant son
narrative_blocks.body: Elsie Ann Jolly Ellis died February 26, 1885 at twenty-six — almost the same young age at which John Ellis's first wife Lois Olive Hibbard had died fourteen years earlier. She left their only child John Clarence at just over a year old. Both of John's young wives are buried in Crawford County Pennsylvania but in separate cemeteries — Elsie at McDowell Dicksonburg, Lois at Greendale Meadville.

burialCemetery.name: Conneautville Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Conneautville Cemetery, Conneautville Crawford County PA. Burial site of John Clarence Ellis (HD4274, gen 8) and likely multiple Ellis descendants. Located in northwestern Crawford County, north of Meadville.
